result.markdown:
--- title: "The QMD File" sidebar: tips-sidebar --- A `.qmd` file is a plain text file combining **Markdown** for prose and **code chunks** for computation. This chapter covers the options that give you precise control over how code runs and how content is shown. --- ## Code chunk options Every code chunk can be fine-tuned with options written as `#|` comments at the top of the chunk: ````markdown ::: {#ce63c222 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Hello! ``` ::: ::: ```` ### Option reference | Option | Values | What it controls | |---|---|---| | `echo` | `true` / `false` / `fenced` | Show the source code | | `eval` | `true` / `false` | Actually run the code | | `output` | `true` / `false` / `asis` | Show the output | | `warning` | `true` / `false` | Show warnings | | `error` | `true` / `false` | Show errors (instead of stopping) | | `include` | `true` / `false` | Include chunk at all (shorthand for echo+output: false) | | `code-fold` | `true` / `false` | Collapse code into a toggle | | `code-summary` | string | Label for the fold toggle | | `fig-cap` | string | Caption for figures | | `fig-align` | `left` / `center` / `right` | Figure alignment | | `label` | string (must start with `fig-` or `tbl-`) | ID for cross-referencing | ### Common combinations **Show output only — hide code:** ```{.yaml} #| echo: false ``` **Show code but don't run it:** ```{.yaml} #| eval: false ``` **Collapsible code block:** ```{.yaml} #| code-fold: true #| code-summary: "Show the code" ``` --- ## Global chunk defaults Set defaults for all chunks in `_quarto.yml` so you don't repeat yourself: ```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"} execute: echo: false warning: false ``` Override for individual chunks as needed. --- ## Conditional content Show or hide content depending on the active **profile** or **format**: ### Profile-based content Use a custom div with a CSS class and conditionally include it: ````markdown ::: {.content-visible when-profile="int"} This paragraph only appears in the **intermediate** profile. ::: ::: {.content-hidden when-profile="beg"} This is hidden in the beginner profile, visible everywhere else. ::: ```` ### Format-based content ````markdown ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} This only appears in HTML output. ::: ::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"} This only appears in PDF output. ::: ```` --- ## Cross-referencing Label a figure, table, or section — then reference it anywhere on the same page. ### Figures ````markdown ::: {#cell-fig-sine .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import numpy as np x = np.linspace(0, 2*np.pi, 200) plt.plot(x, np.sin(x)) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![A plot of sin(x)](exp_qmd_files/figure-html/fig-sine-output-1.png){#fig-sine width=590 height=411} ::: ::: As shown in @fig-sine, the sine function... ```` ### Tables ````markdown ::: {#tbl-data .cell tbl-cap='Summary statistics' execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd df = pd.DataFrame({"x": [1,2,3], "y": [4,5,6]}) df ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
x y
0 1 4
1 2 5
2 3 6
``` ::: ::: The values in @tbl-data show... ```` ### Sections ```{.markdown} ## Introduction {#sec-intro} As described in @sec-intro... ``` ::: {.callout-tip} ## Label naming rules - Figure labels must start with `fig-` - Table labels must start with `tbl-` - Section labels must start with `sec-` ::: --- ## Custom divs and spans Apply any CSS class to a block of content using Quarto's div syntax: ```{.markdown} ::: {.my-class} This block gets the `my-class` CSS class. ::: ``` For inline styling, use a span: ```{.markdown} This word is [highlighted]{.my-highlight} in the text. ``` ### Built-in Bootstrap utility classes These work out of the box without any custom CSS: | Class | Effect | |---|---| | `.text-center` | Centre-align text | | `.text-muted` | Grey text | | `.fw-bold` | Bold text | | `.border` | Add a border | | `.bg-light` | Light background | | `.p-3` | Add padding | | `.mt-4` | Add top margin | Example: ```{.markdown} ::: {.border .p-3 .bg-light} This box has a border, padding, and a light background — no custom CSS needed. ::: ``` --- ## Callout blocks Quarto provides five callout types out of the box: ```{.markdown} ::: {.callout-note} ## Optional title Informational note. ::: ::: {.callout-tip} Practical advice. ::: ::: {.callout-warning} Something to watch out for. ::: ::: {.callout-important} Critical information. ::: ::: {.callout-caution} Proceed carefully. ::: ``` Add `collapse="true"` to make a callout collapsible: ```{.markdown} ::: {.callout-note collapse="true"} ## Click to expand Hidden detail. ::: ```
